name: NetworkX (Graph Analysis)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 3-Clause); standard Python graph library

performance.notes: Pure Python — slow for large graphs (>100K nodes); use cuGraph for GPU acceleration

agent_readiness.notes: Standard Python graph library. Undirected, directed, multigraphs. Classic algorithms: BFS/DFS, shortest paths (Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford), minimum spanning trees, centrality measures, clustering, community detection, graph generators. NetworkX graphs as Python objects (nodes/edges as dicts). Visualization via matplotlib/graphviz. Graph ML with PyG/DGL. Widely used for knowledge graphs, social network analysis, dependency resolution.

gotchas: Pure Python — slow for graphs with millions of nodes/edges
Not suitable for real-time graph computation
Graph algorithms have varying complexity — check docs for each
best_when: General graph analysis and algorithm research
Social network analysis and knowledge graphs
Any Python graph problem under 1M nodes
avoid_when: Billion-node graphs (use cuGraph, GraphX, or Neo4j)
summary: NetworkX is Python's standard graph library. Directed, undirected, and multigraphs. Comprehensive algorithm library: shortest paths, centrality, community detection, clustering, MST. Dict-based node/edge attributes. Excellent documentation. Best for graph analysis under 1M nodes. Pure Python — slow for large graphs; use cuGraph (GPU) or igraph (C-backed) for performance.
